title: Tony Hoare, Turing Award-Winning Computer Scientist Behind QuickSort, Dies At 92
author: BeauHD
summary: Tony Hoare, the Turing Award-winning pioneer who created the Quicksort algorithm, developed Hoare logic, and advanced theories of concurrency and structured programming, has died at age 92. News of his passing was shared today in a blog post. The site I Programmer also commemorated Hoare in a post highlighting his contributions to computer science and the lasting impact of his work. Personal accounts have been shared on Hacker News and Reddit. Many Slashdotters may know Hoare for his aphorism regarding software design: "There are two ways of constructing a software design: One way is to make it so simple that there are obviously no deficiencies, and the other way is to make it so complicated that there are no obvious deficiencies. The first method is far more difficult."
full_text:
Tony Hoare

, the Turing Award-winning pioneer who created the Quicksort

 algorithm, developed Hoare logic

, and advanced theories of concurrency and structured programming, has died at age 92.
 

News of his passing was shared today in a blog post

. The site I Programmer also commemorated Hoare

 in a post highlighting his contributions to computer science and the lasting impact of his work. Personal accounts have been shared on Hacker News

 and Reddit

.
 

Many Slashdotters may know Hoare for his aphorism regarding software design: "There are two ways of constructing a software design: One way is to make it so simple that there are obviously no deficiencies, and the other way is to make it so complicated that there are no obvious deficiencies. The first method is far more difficult."
